sutta:
once available ananda while he dwelt at kosambi in gosita addressed the monk saying references reverend sir they rejoined and he said it is wonderful amazing raven says how this way of escape from the nose awoke within the exalted 1 noah seer arahan wholly awakened to purge man pass by grief and lamentation bring ill and unhappiness to an end pursue the way and realize the cool there will be just the eye but no sensing of objects and the sphere thereof there will be the ear but no sensing of sounds and the sphere thereof there will be the nose but no sensing of smells and the sphere thereof there will be the tongue but no sensing or stays and there's fear thereof there will be the body but no sensing of touch and the sphere thereof now when he had spoken thus the variable udain said to him is then he who senses not a sphere percipient reverend ananda or impercipian and verbal ananda said he is certainly persipient reverend sir and udain asked again but how can he be percipient and yet not sense that sphere and verbal ananda explain considers among who passing wholly beyond form perception bringing to an end perception reaction inattentive to the diverse perceptions enters and abides in the sphere of infinite space thinking space is infinite he is thus percipient but sense is not that sphere again consider considered among who passing wholly beyond the sphere of space infinite space enters and abides in the sphere of infinite consciousness thinking consciousness is infinite he is thus percipient but sense is not that sphere then consider the monk who passing wholly beyond the sphere of infinite consciousness enters and abides in the sphere of nothingness thinking there is nothing he is thus percipient but senses not that sphere now at 1 time robin says i dwelled in the deer pong at anjana grove near saketa and there a nun of jatilla gaha visited me saluted and stood at 1 side thus standing she said to me lord ananda this concentration which is neither bent away nor bent aside in which the restraint is not controlled by conscious effort but by its freedom is stable but by its freedom is steadfast by its state fastness it is content by its content it is not disturbed this concentration lord ananda what is its fruit said to be by the exalted 1 and when she had thus spoken i replied this concentration sister which is neither bent away nor bent aside in which the restraint is not controlled by conscious effort but by its freedom is steadfast by its steadfastness it is content by its content it is not disturbed this concentration sister is said by the exalted 1 to have knowledge as its fruit he is thus persipient references but he senses not that sphere unless the end of the sutra this verifies that when a monk enters the arupa jaanas the mind opens up now and the 5 sense doors are totally close uh when a monk enters the rupa jana states he cannot sense forms sounds smells taste and touch this is a very high state arupa jana state and is said in some other suta when among abides in this arupa chana state na mara cannot see that person in that arupa jana state totally marak satan cannot see him at all in the rupa jana staten he is beyond the reach of mahara beyond the reach of satan but in the arupa jana state is not only beyond the reach mara cannot see him at all because his mind opens up and so he is conscious he's percipient that the mind is like boundless space or like boundless consciousness yet he's not aware of the world around him the normal world of the 5 senses now this lastly the bible ananda is mentioned that none of the uh i think is a jatilla segment came to ask him about this concentration which is neither benta wind or benticide in which the restraint is not controlled by conscious effort this is something very interesting is mentioned earlier we already saw it earlier in some other suta that in the state of china the restraint is not controlled by conscious effort restraint of what restraint of the hindrances you don't make and don't need to make an effort now to restrain the hindrances and vietnam they are automatically restrained and then it is by its freedom it is steadfast by its state fastness it is content and because of its content it is not disturbed and this kind of concentration has knowledge as its fruit that means liberation destruction of the earth of us now there is another suta i'd like to mention in the sanguita nikaya 22.53 where the suta part of the suta reached as follows if attachment for the element of body feeling perception volition consciousness the 5 aggregates monks is abandoned by a monk by that abandonment of attachment its object is cut off and there is no support for consciousness that unsupported consciousness has no increase and is without conditioners released by its release of freedom it is steadfast by its discontent by its content it is not disturbed not being disturbed it just by itself attains nirvana this is quite interesting uh this saying uh that when attachment for the 5 aggregates uh these 5 things that we associate with the self is abandoned is let go by uh by among for consciousness or the support for consciousness is cut off and and here the buddha mentions about a type of consciousness which is not the normal consciousness unsupported consciousness this is very interesting because it talks about this type of consciousness which is seldom seen in the sutasla unsupported consciousness has no increase is without conditioners and it is released by its release it is steadfast by its state fastness it is content but its content it is not disturbed not being disturbed it just by itself it attains nirvana so this is 1 of the sutas where there are some monks who are of the opinion that even when the 6 normal consciousness dies dies away it stops there is still another type of consciousness similar to this apartheid unsupported consciousness which has no object does not need an object normal sex consciousness must have an object for it to arise the seeing consciousness hearing smelling tasting touching thinking consciousness they need an object for it to arise but this unsupported consciousness and does not need an object ah so it is 1 of the interesting sutras in fact there is uh another suta 1 of the very early sutras in the utah nikaya where the buddha said mentioned something like like when he's supported then he sings and when he strives he also sings or something and then without support and unstriving he cross to the other side so here also it hints about perhaps the unsupported consciousness
